options.text: Deploy shared AVD security infrastructure: a Secrets Key Vault for storing AVD credentials, and an Encryption Key Vault for Customer-Managed Key (CMK) encryption. Run this deployment before deploying host pools, image management, or add-ons that use CMK. The resource IDs output by this deployment are used as inputs to downstream solutions.

elements.name: nameConvResTypeAtEnd
elements.label: Resource Type Abbreviation at End of Name
elements.toolTip: When checked, names follow the pattern 'avd-security-eus-rg'. When unchecked (default), names follow 'rg-avd-security-eus'.

options.text: The Secrets Key Vault (Standard SKU) stores AVD credentials: VM local administrator and domain join account. Downstream deployers reference this vault by resource ID only — they do not need read access to the secrets themselves. To read secrets at deployment time (e.g., for getSecret() ARM references), the deploying identity needs the 'Key Vault Secrets User' role.

elements.name: deploySecretsKeyVault
elements.label: Deploy Secrets Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Deploy a Standard SKU Key Vault for storing AVD credentials.

elements.name: enableSoftDelete
elements.label: Enable Soft Delete
elements.toolTip: Allow recovery of deleted Key Vault objects within the retention period.

elements.name: enablePurgeProtection
elements.label: Enable Purge Protection
elements.toolTip: Prevent permanent deletion of the Key Vault and its objects during the retention period. Strongly recommended for production environments.

elements.name: retentionDays
elements.label: Soft Delete Retention (Days)
elements.toolTip: Number of days to retain soft-deleted Key Vault objects before permanent deletion.

options.text: Provide the AVD session host credentials to store in the Secrets Key Vault. All fields are optional — you can store credentials later by re-running this deployment. Domain join credentials are only required for ADDS or Entra Kerberos hybrid environments.

elements.name: virtualMachineAdminUserName
elements.label: Admin Username
elements.toolTip: Local administrator username for AVD session hosts. Stored as 'VirtualMachineAdminUserName' in the Key Vault.

constraints.validationMessage: Must start with a letter. Alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens allowed. Maximum 20 characters.

elements.name: virtualMachineAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Local administrator password for AVD session hosts. Stored as 'VirtualMachineAdminPassword' in the Key Vault. Must meet Windows password complexity requirements.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 12-123 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, a digit, and a special character.

elements.name: domainJoinUserPrincipalName
elements.label: Domain Join UPN
elements.toolTip: UPN of the privileged account used to domain join session hosts. Stored as 'DomainJoinUserPrincipalName' in the Key Vault.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid UPN format (user@domain.tld).

elements.name: domainJoinUserPassword
elements.toolTip: Password for the domain join account. Stored as 'DomainJoinUserPassword' in the Key Vault.

options.text: The Encryption Key Vault (Premium SKU) stores RSA-4096 keys for Customer-Managed Key (CMK) encryption of VM disks, FSLogix storage, image management storage, image build storage, and function app storage. Required when any AVD solution uses CMK. The deploying identity requires the 'Key Vault Crypto Officer' role on this vault at deployment time to create encryption keys.

elements.name: deployEncryptionKeyVault
elements.label: Deploy Encryption Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Deploy a Premium SKU Key Vault (HSM-backed) for Customer-Managed Key encryption across all AVD solutions.

options.text: Configure private endpoints to disable public network access on the Key Vaults and route all traffic over the Microsoft backbone. Requires an existing virtual network with a dedicated subnet (no delegations) and a Private DNS Zone for Key Vault (privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net).

elements.name: privateEndpoint
elements.label: Deploy Private Endpoints
elements.toolTip: When enabled, public network access is disabled on both Key Vaults and private endpoints are deployed into the selected subnet.

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network containing the private endpoint subnet. Only virtual networks in the selected region are shown.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').networkSection.virtualNetworksApi.value, (vnet) => equals(vnet.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (vnet) => parse(concat('{"label":"', vnet.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(vnet.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"name":"', vnet.name, '","id":"', vnet.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: privateEndpointSubnet
elements.label: Private Endpoint Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select the subnet for Key Vault private endpoints. Must not have any service delegations.

elements.name: enablePrivateDNSIntegration
elements.label: Enable Private DNS Zone Integration
elements.toolTip: Automatically register Key Vault private endpoints with an Azure Private DNS Zone for proper name resolution.

elements.name: privateDNSZonesSubscription
elements.label: Private DNS Zone Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription that contains your Private DNS Zones. In hub-and-spoke topologies this is typically the hub/connectivity subscription.

elements.name: keyVaultPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Key Vault Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the Private DNS Zone for Key Vault (privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net or privatelink.vaultcore.usgovcloudapi.net for Azure Government).

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').privateDnsSection.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (zone) => contains(zone.name, 'vaultcore')), (zone) => parse(concat('{"label":"', zone.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(zone.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', zone.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Optionally send Key Vault audit and diagnostic logs to an existing Log Analytics Workspace. Key Vault logs capture secret access, key operations, and access control changes — security and compliance telemetry best suited for a security-team-owned workspace or SIEM.

elements.name: enableMonitoring
elements.label: Send Key Vault Diagnostic Logs to Log Analytics

elements.name: monitoringSubscription
elements.label: Log Analytics Workspace Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the target Log Analytics Workspace.

elements.name: logAnalyticsWorkspace
elements.label: Log Analytics Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select the Log Analytics Workspace for Key Vault diagnostic logs.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('monitoring').logAnalyticsWorkspacesApi.value, (la) => parse(concat('{"label":"', la.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(la.id, '/'), 4)), ' | Location: ', la.location, '","value":"', la.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Apply tags to the deployed resources. Tags are applied per resource type.
